samples.xml_description: Implement a module for pulse detection. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). One-bit input signal. Output signal indicating the presence of pulses. Initial state of the pulse detection process. State indicating that a pulse is detected. State indicating that a pulse is detected. State indicating the end of a pulse. Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process. Initialize state to S0. state = S0; State transition logic. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin state <= S0; end else begin case (state) S0: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; end else begin state <= S0; end end S1: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S2; end else begin state <= S0; end end S2: begin if (~data_in) begin state <= S3; end else begin state <= S2; end end S3: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; end else begin state <= S0; end end default: state <= S0; endcase end end Declare a register data_out to keep track of the output signal. Initialize data_out to 0. data_out = 1'b0; Output logic. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin case (state) S0: data_out <= 1'b0; S1: data_out <= 1'b0; S2: data_out <= 1'b0; S3: data_out <= 1'b1; default: data_out <= 1'b0; endcase end end Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process. State transition logic: Whenever the clock signal transitions from low to high (positive edge) or the reset signal transitions from high to low (negative edge), the state should be updated based on the current state and the value of data_in. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, set the state to the initial state (S0). Otherwise, use a case statement to determine the next state based on the current state and the value of data_in. If the current state is S0 and data_in is 1, the next state is S1. If data_in is 0, the next state is S0. If the current state is S1 and data_in is 1, the next state is S2. If data_in is 0, the next state is S0. If the current state is S2 and data_in is 0, the next state is S3. If data_in is 1, the next state is S2. If the current state is S3 and data_in is 1, the next state is S1. If data_in is 0, the next state is S0. Declare a register data_out to keep track of the output signal. Output logic: Whenever the clock signal transitions from low to high (positive edge) or the reset signal transitions from high to low (negative edge), the data_out should be updated based on the current state. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, set the data_out to 0. Otherwise, use a case statement to determine the next value of data_out based on the current state. If the current state is S0, data_out is 0. If the current state is S1, data_out is 0. If the current state is S2, data_out is 0. If the current state is S3, data_out is 1.
